criteriaResults.evidence: 'mailctl --help' lists 'doctor' as a top-level command alongside compose, reply, forward. 'mailctl doctor' with mocked all-passing checks exits 0. Output shows 5 checkmark indicators (✔). All 3 TestDoctorCommandExists tests pass.

check_osascript() returns status='pass' when subprocess.run returns rc=0. Returns status='fail' with 'Install Xcode command-line tools: xcode-select --install' when FileNotFoundError is raised or when returncode != 0. All 3 TestDoctorCheckOsascript tests pass.

check_mail_installed() checks os.path.isdir for /System/Applications/Mail.app and /Applications/Mail.app. Returns 'pass' when found, 'fail' with 'Mail.app is not installed' message when not. Both TestDoctorCheckMailInstalled tests pass.

check_mail_running() queries System Events via osascript. Returns 'pass' when 'true' in stdout. Returns 'fail' with 'Launch Mail.app and try again: open -a Mail' when not running. Both TestDoctorCheckMailRunning tests pass.

check_scriptable() runs 'tell application "Mail" to name' via osascript. Returns 'pass' when rc=0. Returns 'fail' with 'Grant access in System Settings > Privacy & Security > Automation.' when 'not authorized' in stderr. Both TestDoctorCheckScriptable tests pass.

check_accounts() queries Mail.app for account count. Returns 'pass' with '2 account(s) configured' when count > 0. Returns 'fail' with 'No email accounts configured' and 'Add an account in Mail.app > Settings > Accounts' when count == 0. Both TestDoctorCheckAccounts tests pass.

Doctor runs ALL_CHECKS sequentially regardless of failures. test_no_short_circuit_on_failure mocks 3 failures + 2 passes and verifies all 5 are listed with ✔/✘ indicators. test_passing_checks_alongside_failures verifies total ✔+✘ >= 5. All 3 TestDoctorOutputFormat tests pass.

Doctor exits EXIT_SUCCESS (0) when all pass, EXIT_GENERAL_ERROR (1) when any fail. Verified via test_exit_zero_all_pass, test_exit_one_on_single_failure, test_exit_one_on_multiple_failures. Real execution against system also shows exit code 1 when mail_running check fails. All 3 TestDoctorExitCodes tests pass.

'mailctl doctor --json' outputs valid JSON with 'all_passed' (boolean) and 'checks' (array of objects with name/status/message). Real execution confirmed: JSON output has 5 checks, each with name, status ('pass'/'fail'), and message. test_json_all_pass, test_json_with_failure, test_json_check_structure all pass.

'mailctl doctor --help' output says 'Diagnose Mail.app integration. Checks that osascript is installed, Mail.app is present and running, automation permissions are granted, and accounts are configured.' and lists '--json' as an option. Both TestDoctorHelp tests pass.

Code inspection of errors.py: MailNotRunningError has 'Mail.app is not running. Launch Mail.app and try again.' (problem + solution). PermissionDeniedError has 'Automation permission denied. Grant access in System Settings > Privacy & Security > Automation.' (problem + solution). ScriptTimeoutError has 'AppleScript timed out after Xs. Mail.app may be unresponsive — try restarting Mail.app or check whether it is stuck on a dialog.' (problem + solution). render_error in output.py prefixes 'Error:'. All 3 TestErrorFormatConsistency tests pass.

Code inspection: all command files use render_error() for user-input validation errors (bad arguments, missing required options, mutually exclusive flags) and exit with EXIT_USAGE_ERROR (2). Runtime/AppleScript errors use handle_mail_error() which exits with EXIT_GENERAL_ERROR (1). Grep for 'err_console.print' and 'typer.echo.*Error' in commands/ returned zero matches — no ad-hoc error printing. Commands verified: compose.py, reply_forward.py, messages.py, mailboxes.py, mark_move.py, delete.py, drafts.py, doctor.py.

test_error_polish.py tests 3 commands: messages list (test_messages_list_account_not_found), mailboxes list (test_mailboxes_list_account_not_found), compose (test_compose_from_account_not_found). All produce 'not found' in stderr and list known accounts (PersonalAccount/WorkAccount). All 3 TestAccountNotFound tests pass.

messages.py line 784-790: mailbox not found produces 'Mailbox "<name>" not found. Use mailctl mailboxes list to see available mailboxes.' mark_move.py line 496-503: same pattern for move. test_messages_list_mailbox_not_found and test_messages_move_mailbox_not_found both pass with correct error patterns.

Tested across 3 commands: messages show (test_messages_show_not_found), messages mark (test_messages_mark_not_found), messages delete (test_messages_delete_not_found). All produce errors referencing the message ID '99999' and 'not found'. All 3 TestMessageNotFound tests pass.

'messages list' with empty results outputs 'No messages found.' (messages.py line 798). 'messages search' with empty results outputs 'No messages matched your search.' (messages.py line 889). Both TestEmptyResults tests pass.

test_compose_no_body_error invokes compose with --to and --subject but no body source. Error message mentions at least 2 of: --body, --body-file, stdin. Test asserts methods_mentioned >= 2 and passes.

test_compose_attach_not_found invokes compose with --attach /nonexistent/file.pdf. Error contains the exact path '/nonexistent/file.pdf' and 'not'+'exist' or 'found'. Test passes. compose.py line 500: render_error("Attachment '{path}' does not exist or is not a file.").

'mailctl --no-color doctor' tested both in unit tests and against real system. test_no_color_no_ansi asserts no '\x1b[' or '\x1b' in output. test_no_color_still_readable verifies checkmarks and content still present. Real execution via 'cat -v' confirmed no ANSI escape sequences — only UTF-8 Unicode symbols. Both tests pass.

src/mailctl/commands/doctor.py exists with register() function. cli.py line 83: 'from mailctl.commands.doctor import register as register_doctor'. cli.py line 98: 'register_doctor(app)' — registered on top-level app, not a subgroup. Individual check functions (check_osascript, check_mail_installed, check_mail_running, check_scriptable, check_accounts) are independently callable/mockable. Uses render_error from mailctl.output. All 4 TestDoctorArchitecture tests pass.

36 doctor test cases collected in test_doctor.py covering: all-pass (3 tests), osascript-missing/error (3 tests), Mail-not-installed (2 tests), Mail-not-running (2 tests), permission-denied (2 tests), no-accounts (2 tests), output format/no-short-circuit (3 tests), exit codes pass/fail (3 tests), --json pass/fail/structure (3 tests), --help (2 tests), --no-color (2 tests), architecture (4 tests), comprehensive/multiple failures (5 tests). All 36 pass. Threshold of 11 exceeded.

test_error_polish.py has 15 test cases: 3 error format tests (C-230), 3 account-not-found tests across different commands (C-232), 2 mailbox-not-found tests (C-233), 3 message-not-found tests (C-234), 2 empty-results tests (C-235), 1 empty-body test (C-236), 1 attachment-not-found test (C-237). Threshold of 4 error-polish-specific tests exceeded. All 15 pass.
Full test suite: 554 tests collected, 554 passed in 0.50s. Zero failures, zero errors. Includes all Sprint 1-8 tests plus 51 new Sprint 9 tests (36 doctor + 15 error polish).

'mailctl --version' outputs 'mailctl 0.1.0'. 'mailctl --help' lists all command groups: accounts, mailboxes, messages, compose, reply, forward, drafts, and the new doctor command. Full test suite (554 tests) passes with zero failures, confirming all Sprint 1-8 functionality is preserved.

regressionResults.details: All 554 tests pass including all Sprint 1-8 test suites. No test regressions detected. Commands verified: --version, accounts list, mailboxes list, messages list/show/search/mark/move/delete, compose, reply, forward, drafts edit.

holisticScores.codeQualityNotes: Clean module structure following established register() pattern. Individual check functions are independently testable. Dataclass for CheckResult. Consistent error handling through shared render_error/handle_mail_error. No code duplication — reuses compose utilities where needed. Well-structured docstrings throughout.
holisticScores.testCoverageNotes: 51 new tests covering doctor command (36) and error polish (15). Tests cover happy paths, failure paths, multiple simultaneous failures, JSON output, no-color, exit codes, architecture. Full suite of 554 tests passes. All edge cases exercised through mocks.
holisticScores.uxConsistencyNotes: Doctor output uses clear ✔/✘ indicators with color coding. All error messages follow consistent 'Error: <problem>. <solution>.' pattern. --json and --no-color work correctly. Exit codes are consistent (0=success, 1=runtime error, 2=usage error). Empty results produce helpful messages rather than silent output.
holisticScores.safetyModelNotes: Doctor command is entirely read-only — queries system state only, never modifies anything. No new send paths introduced. Error polish changes are string/formatting only — no behavioral changes to safety model. All existing safety tests (test_safety_model.py) continue to pass.
holisticScores.errorHandlingNotes: This sprint's primary focus was error handling polish and it delivers well. Consistent render_error usage across all commands. Account-not-found errors list known accounts. Mailbox-not-found errors suggest 'mailctl mailboxes list'. Message-not-found errors reference the ID. Empty results show helpful messages. Attachment errors include the file path. Doctor checks never short-circuit on failure.

summary: Sprint 9 delivers a comprehensive 'mailctl doctor' diagnostic command and polishes error messages across all existing commands. The doctor command implements 5 independent checks (osascript availability, Mail.app installed, Mail.app running, automation permissions, accounts configured) with clear pass/fail indicators, JSON output, --no-color support, and correct exit codes. Error messages across all commands now follow a consistent 'Error: <problem>. <solution>.' format with actionable guidance. All 24 criteria pass. Full test suite of 554 tests passes with zero failures.
